growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Laguna Chitin (LC) mineral medium with amorphous chitin

up.units: g/L (substrate supplied, completely degraded in <2 wk)
not quantified (baseline; coculture consumed O2 ~2x faster than axenic host, P<=0.009)

sec.method: HPAEC, axenic host growth on chitin

growth_records.cite: La Cono et al. Symbiosis between nanohaloarchaeon and haloarchaeon is based on utilization of different polysaccharides. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2007232117. [PMC7443923]
growth_records.snippet: neither the maximum specific growth rates (0.028 to 0.035 h-1) nor the yield of the host biomass were affected ... acetate (6.39 +/- 1.55 vs. 2.10 +/- 0.33 mmol) ... GlcNAc ... 3.71 +/- 0.90 and 5.20 +/- 1.61 mmol
growth_records.notes: Archaeon (prokaryote). mu_max reported range 0.028-0.035 h-1 (chitin, applies to both axenic host and coculture). Doubling time td=ln2/mu: 0.693/0.035=19.8 h to 0.693/0.028=24.8 h. Acetate 2.10+/-0.33 mmol and GlcNAc 3.71+/-0.90 mmol are supernatant amounts (not per-gDW rates); no biomass concentration given so qP/qS not derivable. Full LC ionic composition is in SI Appendix Table S1 (not in main text); listed components are those stated in text (240 g/L total salts, 3.42 M Na+, 0.37 M Mg2+, chitin 2 g/L). T=40C, pH 7.0.

growth_records.medium: Laguna Chitin (LC) mineral medium with amorphous chitin

up.units: g/L (substrate supplied, completely degraded in <2 wk)
~2x faster than axenic host (P<=0.009); not absolutely quantified

sec.method: HPAEC; 3-fold higher than axenic host (2.10 mmol), P=0.002

growth_records.cite: La Cono et al. Symbiosis between nanohaloarchaeon and haloarchaeon is based on utilization of different polysaccharides. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2007232117. [PMC7443923]
growth_records.snippet: the growth of host-ectosymbiont association on chitin was accompanied by a three-fold higher accumulation of acetate (6.39 +/- 1.55 vs. 2.10 +/- 0.33 mmol, P = 0.002) ... consumed oxygen almost twice as fast as the pure Halomicrobium cultur
growth_records.notes: Coculture of two archaea (prokaryotes). mu_max on chitin 0.028-0.035 h-1 (unchanged vs axenic host). td=ln2/mu = 19.8-24.8 h. Acetate 6.39+/-1.55 mmol and GlcNAc 5.20+/-1.61 mmol are supernatant amounts, not per-gDW rates; no biomass conc reported so qP not derivable. O2 uptake ~2x host but not absolutely quantified. Coculture requires microoxic O2 and >=75 mM Mg2+; upper Mg2+ limit for LC1Nh ~800 mM.

growth_records.medium: Laguna Chitin (LC) mineral medium with starch

up.units: consumed (hydrolyzed to glucose); amount not stated

sec.method: HPAEC; only detected hydrolysis product, no oligomers

growth_records.cite: La Cono et al. Symbiosis between nanohaloarchaeon and haloarchaeon is based on utilization of different polysaccharides. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2007232117. [PMC7443923]
growth_records.snippet: the host-ectosymbiont coculture grew rapidly on the alpha-glucans, starch, and glycogen. The growth rates on these two substrates were comparable to (mu_max in the range of 0.043 to 0.066 h-1) ... the only detected hydrolysis product was mo
growth_records.notes: Coculture of two archaea; Halomicrobium LC1Hm alone cannot grow on starch (host gains this capacity via the nanohaloarchaeon LC1Nh). mu_max range 0.043-0.066 h-1 spans both starch and glycogen. td=ln2/mu: 0.693/0.066=10.5 h to 0.693/0.043=16.1 h. Glucose <=4.61+/-0.81 mmol is a supernatant amount, not a per-gDW rate. Starch concentration not stated in text.

growth_records.medium: Laguna Chitin (LC) mineral medium with glycogen

up.units: consumed (hydrolyzed to glucose); amount not stated

sec.units: monomeric glucose released (glycogen hydrolysis product); amount not separately stated

growth_records.cite: La Cono et al. Symbiosis between nanohaloarchaeon and haloarchaeon is based on utilization of different polysaccharides. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2007232117. [PMC7443923]
growth_records.snippet: the host-ectosymbiont coculture grew rapidly on the alpha-glucans, starch, and glycogen. The growth rates on these two substrates were comparable to (mu_max in the range of 0.043 to 0.066 h-1)
growth_records.notes: Coculture of two archaea; Halomicrobium LC1Hm alone cannot grow on glycogen (gains capacity via LC1Nh). mu_max range 0.043-0.066 h-1 covers both glycogen and starch. td=ln2/mu = 10.5-16.1 h. Glucose is the sole hydrolysis product but a glycogen-specific supernatant amount is not separately reported (starch value <=4.61 mmol given). Glycogen concentration not stated in text.
